Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “For purposes of this section, the term ‘qualified rollover contribution’ means a rollover contribution—Pub. L. 110–245, § 109(b)section 824(a) of Pub. L. 109–280“(1) to a Roth IRA from another such account,“(2) from an eligible retirement plan, but only if—“(A) in the case of an individual retirement plan, such rollover contribution meets the requirements of section 408(d)(3), and“(B) in the case of any eligible retirement plan (as defined in section 402(c)(8)(B) other than clauses (i) and (ii) thereof), such rollover contribution meets the requirements of section 402(c), 403(b)(8), or 457(e)(16), as applicable.For purposes of section 408(d)(3)(B), there shall be disregarded any qualified rollover contribution from an individual retirement plan (other than a Roth IRA) to a Roth IRA.”, amended subsec. (e), as in effect before amendment by , by reenacting heading without change and amending text to read as follows: “For purposes of this section—Pub. L. 110–245, § 109(a)section 824(a) of Pub. L. 109–280“(1) .—The term ‘qualified rollover contribution’ means a rollover contribution to a Roth IRA from another such account, or from an individual retirement plan, but only if such rollover contribution meets the requirements of section 408(d)(3). Such term includes a rollover contribution described in section 402A(c)(3)(A). For purposes of section 408(d)(3)(B), there shall be disregarded any qualified rollover contribution from an individual retirement plan (other than a Roth IRA) to a Roth IRA.In general“(2)Military death gratuity.—“(A) .—The term ‘qualified rollover contribution’ includes a contribution to a Roth IRA maintained for the benefit of an individual made before the end of the 1-year period beginning on the date on which such individual receives an amount under , United States Code, or of such Code, with respect to a person, to the extent that such contribution does not exceed—In generalsection 1477 of title 10section 1967 of title 38“(i) the sum of the amounts received during such period by such individual under such sections with respect to such person, reduced by“(ii) the amounts so received which were contributed to a Coverdell education savings account under section 530(d)(9).“(B) .—Section 408(d)(3)(B) shall not apply with respect to amounts treated as a rollover by subparagraph (A).Annual limit on number of rollovers not to apply“(C) 72.—For purposes of applying section 72 in the case of a distribution which is not a qualified distribution, the amount treated as a rollover by reason of subparagraph (A) shall be treated as investment in the contract.” See 2006 Amendment note below.Application of section2006—Subsec. (c)(3)(B). , redesignated subpar. (C) as (B) and struck out former subpar. (B). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “A taxpayer shall not be allowed to make a qualified rollover contribution to a Roth IRA from an individual retirement plan other than a Roth IRA during any taxable year if, for the taxable year of the distribution to which such contribution relates—Pub. L. 109–222, § 512(a)(1)“(i) the taxpayer’s adjusted gross income exceeds $100,000, or“(ii) the taxpayer is a married individual filing a separate return.This subparagraph shall not apply to a qualified rollover contribution from a Roth IRA or to a qualified rollover contribution from a designated Roth account which is a rollover contribution described in section 402A(c)(3)(A).” See Effective Date of 2006 Amendment note below., substituted “eligible retirement plan” for “IRA” in heading and “an eligible retirement plan (as defined by section 402(c)(8)(B))” for “individual retirement plan” in introductory provisions. Rollover of Amounts Received in Airline Carrier Bankruptcy to Roth IRAs, , , provided that:Pub. L. 110–458, title I, § 125122 Stat. 5115Dec. 23, 2008“(a).—General RuleIf a qualified airline employee receives any airline payment amount and transfers any portion of such amount to a Roth IRA within 180 days of receipt of such amount (or, if later, within 180 days of the date of the enactment of this Act []), then such amount (to the extent so transferred) shall be treated as a qualified rollover contribution described in section 408A(e) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, and the limitations described in section 408A(c)(3) of such Code shall not apply to any such transfer.Dec. 23, 2008“(b).—Definitions and Special RulesFor purposes of this section—“(1)Airline payment amount.—“(A).—In generalThe term ‘airline payment amount’ means any payment of any money or other property which is payable by a commercial passenger airline carrier to a qualified airline employee—“(i)under the approval of an order of a Federal bankruptcy court in a case filed after , and before , andSeptember 11, 2001January 1, 2007“(ii)in respect of the qualified airline employee’s interest in a bankruptcy claim against the carrier, any note of the carrier (or amount paid in lieu of a note being issued), or any other fixed obligation of the carrier to pay a lump sum amount.The amount of such payment shall be determined without regard to any requirement to deduct and withhold tax from such payment under sections 3102(a) and 3402(a).“(B).—ExceptionAn airline payment amount shall not include any amount payable on the basis of the carrier’s future earnings or profits.“(2).—Qualified airline employeeThe term ‘qualified airline employee’ means an employee or former employee of a commercial passenger airline carrier who was a participant in a defined benefit plan maintained by the carrier which—“(A)is a plan described in section 401(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 which includes a trust exempt from tax under section 501(a) of such Code, and“(B)was terminated or became subject to the restrictions contained in paragraphs (2) and (3) of section 402(b) of the Pension Protection Act of 2006 [, note].Pub. L. 109–28026 U.S.C. 430“(3).—Reporting requirementsIf a commercial passenger airline carrier pays 1 or more airline payment amounts, the carrier shall, within 90 days of such payment (or, if later, within 90 days of the date of the enactment of this Act []), report—Dec. 23, 2008“(A)to the Secretary of the Treasury, the names of the qualified airline employees to whom such amounts were paid, and“(B)to the Secretary and to such employees, the years and the amounts of the payments.Such reports shall be in such form, and contain such additional information, as the Secretary may prescribe.“(c).—Effective DateThis section shall apply to transfers made after the date of the enactment of this Act [] with respect to airline payment amounts paid before, on, or after such date.”Dec. 23, 2008

For purposes of section 408(d)(3)(B), there shall be disregarded any qualified rollover contribution from an individual retirement plan (other than a Roth IRA) to a Roth IRA.In general“(2)Military death gratuity.—“(A) .—The term ‘qualified rollover contribution’ includes a contribution to a Roth IRA maintained for the benefit of an individual made before the end of the 1-year period beginning on the date on which such individual receives an amount under , United States Code, or of such Code, with respect to a person, to the extent that such contribution does not exceed—In generalsection 1477 of title 10section 1967 of title 38“(i) the sum of the amounts received during such period by such individual under such sections with respect to such person, reduced by“(ii) the amounts so received which were contributed to a Coverdell education savings account under section 530(d)(9).“(B) .—Section 408(d)(3)(B) shall not apply with respect to amounts treated as a rollover by subparagraph (A).Annual limit on number of rollovers not to apply“(C) 72.—For purposes of applying section 72 in the case of a distribution which is not a qualified distribution, the amount treated as a rollover by reason of subparagraph (A) shall be treated as investment in the contract.” See 2006 Amendment note below.Application of section2006—Subsec. Rollover of Amounts Received in Airline Carrier Bankruptcy to Roth IRAs, , , provided that:Pub. L. 110–458, title I, § 125122 Stat. 5115Dec. 23, 2008“(a).—General RuleIf a qualified airline employee receives any airline payment amount and transfers any portion of such amount to a Roth IRA within 180 days of receipt of such amount (or, if later, within 180 days of the date of the enactment of this Act []), then such amount (to the extent so transferred) shall be treated as a qualified rollover contribution described in section 408A(e) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, and the limitations described in section 408A(c)(3) of such Code shall not apply to any such transfer.Dec. 23, 2008“(b).—Definitions and Special RulesFor purposes of this section—“(1)Airline payment amount.—“(A).—In generalThe term ‘airline payment amount’ means any payment of any money or other property which is payable by a commercial passenger airline carrier to a qualified airline employee—“(i)under the approval of an order of a Federal bankruptcy court in a case filed after , and before , andSeptember 11, 2001January 1, 2007“(ii)in respect of the qualified airline employee’s interest in a bankruptcy claim against the carrier, any note of the carrier (or amount paid in lieu of a note being issued), or any other fixed obligation of the carrier to pay a lump sum amount.The amount of such payment shall be determined without regard to any requirement to deduct and withhold tax from such payment under sections 3102(a) and 3402(a).“(B).—ExceptionAn airline payment amount shall not include any amount payable on the basis of the carrier’s future earnings or profits.“(2).—Qualified airline employeeThe term ‘qualified airline employee’ means an employee or former employee of a commercial passenger airline carrier who was a participant in a defined benefit plan maintained by the carrier which—“(A)is a plan described in section 401(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 which includes a trust exempt from tax under section 501(a) of such Code, and“(B)was terminated or became subject to the restrictions contained in paragraphs (2) and (3) of section 402(b) of the Pension Protection Act of 2006 [, note].Pub. L. 109–28026 U.S.C. 430“(3).—Reporting requirementsIf a commercial passenger airline carrier pays 1 or more airline payment amounts, the carrier shall, within 90 days of such payment (or, if later, within 90 days of the date of the enactment of this Act []), report—Dec. 23, 2008“(A)to the Secretary of the Treasury, the names of the qualified airline employees to whom such amounts were paid, and“(B)to the Secretary and to such employees, the years and the amounts of the payments.Such reports shall be in such form, and contain such additional information, as the Secretary may prescribe.“(c).—Effective DateThis section shall apply to transfers made after the date of the enactment of this Act [] with respect to airline payment amounts paid before, on, or after such date.”Dec. 23, 2008

children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: Except as provided in this section, a Roth IRA shall be treated for purposes of this title in the same manner as an individual retirement plan.

children.heading: Roth IRA
children.label: b Roth IRA
children.text: For purposes of this title, the term “Roth IRA” means an individual retirement plan (as defined in section 7701(a)(37)) which is designated (in such manner as the Secretary may prescribe) at the time of establishment of the plan as a Roth IRA. Such designation shall be made in such manner as the Secretary may prescribe.

children.heading: No deduction allowed
children.label: 1 No deduction allowed
children.text: No deduction shall be allowed under section 219 for a contribution to a Roth IRA.

children.label: A
children.text: the maximum amount allowable as a deduction under section 219 with respect to such individual for such taxable year (computed without regard to subsection (g) of such section), over

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate amount of contributions for such taxable year to all other individual retirement plans (other than Roth IRAs) maintained for the benefit of the individual.

children.heading: Limits based on modified adjusted gross income
children.label: 3 Limits based on modified adjusted gross income
I
children.text: the taxpayer’s adjusted gross income for such taxable year, over

children.label: II
children.text: the applicable dollar amount, bears to

children.label: ii
children.text: $15,000 ($10,000 in the case of a joint return or a married individual filing a separate return).

children.label: i
children.text: adjusted gross income shall be determined in the same manner as under section 219(g)(3), except that any amount included in gross income under subsection (d)(3) shall not be taken into account, and

children.label: I
children.text: in the case of a taxpayer filing a joint return, $150,000,

children.label: II
children.text: in the case of any other taxpayer (other than a married individual filing a separate return), $95,000, and

children.label: III
children.text: in the case of a married individual filing a separate return, zero.

children.heading: Marital status
children.label: C Marital status
children.text: Section 219(g)(4) shall apply for purposes of this paragraph.

children.label: i
children.text: such dollar amount, multiplied by

children.label: ii
children.text: the cost-of-living adjustment determined under section 1(f)(3) for the calendar year in which the taxable year begins, determined by substituting “calendar year 2005” for “calendar year 2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.

children.heading: Special rule for certain transfers from qualified tuition programs
children.label: E Special rule for certain transfers from qualified tuition programs
i
children.text: the amount of contributions described in section 529(c)(3)(E) for the taxable year, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the amount of the reduction determined under such subparagraph (determined without regard to this subparagraph).

children.heading: Mandatory distribution rules not to apply before death
children.label: 4 Mandatory distribution rules not to apply before death
A
children.text: Section 401(a)(9)(A).

children.label: B
children.text: The incidental death benefit requirements of section 401(a).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No rollover contribution may be made to a Roth IRA unless it is a qualified rollover contribution.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: A qualified rollover contribution shall not be taken into account for purposes of paragraph (2).

children.heading: Exception for rollovers from qualified tuition programs
children.label: ii Exception for rollovers from qualified tuition programs
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply to any qualified rollover contribution described in subsection (e)(1)(C).

children.heading: Time when contributions made
children.label: 6 Time when contributions made
children.text: For purposes of this section, the rule of section 219(f)(3) shall apply.

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: 1 Exclusion
children.text: Any qualified distribution from a Roth IRA shall not be includible in gross income.

children.label: i
children.text: made on or after the date on which the individual attains age 59½,

children.label: ii
children.text: made to a beneficiary (or to the estate of the individual) on or after the death of the individual,

children.label: iii
children.text: attributable to the individual’s being disabled (within the meaning of section 72(m)(7)), or

children.label: iv
children.text: which is a qualified special purpose distribution.

children.heading: Distributions within nonexclusion period
children.label: B Distributions within nonexclusion period
children.text: A payment or distribution from a Roth IRA shall not be treated as a qualified distribution under subparagraph (A) if such payment or distribution is made within the 5-taxable year period beginning with the first taxable year for which the individual made a contribution to a Roth IRA (or such individual’s spouse, or employer in the case of a simple retirement account (as defined in section 408(p)) or simplified employee pension (as defined in section 408(k)), made a contribution to a Roth IRA) established for such individual.

children.heading: Distributions of excess contributions and earnings
children.label: C Distributions of excess contributions and earnings
children.text: The term “qualified distribution” shall not include any distribution of any contribution described in section 408(d)(4) and any net income allocable to the contribution.

children.heading: Rollovers from an eligible retirement plan other than a Roth IRA
children.label: 3 Rollovers from an eligible retirement plan other than a Roth IRA
i
children.text: there shall be included in gross income any amount which would be includible were it not part of a qualified rollover contribution,

children.label: ii
children.text: section 72(t) shall not apply, and

children.label: iii
children.text: unless the taxpayer elects not to have this clause apply, any amount required to be included in gross income for any taxable year beginning in 2010 by reason of this paragraph shall be so included ratably over the 2-taxable-year period beginning with the first taxable year beginning in 2011.

children.heading: Distributions to which paragraph applies
children.label: B Distributions to which paragraph applies
children.text: This paragraph shall apply to a distribution from an eligible retirement plan (as defined by section 402(c)(8)(B)) maintained for the benefit of an individual which is contributed to a Roth IRA maintained for the benefit of such individual in a qualified rollover contribution. This paragraph shall not apply to a distribution which is a qualified rollover contribution from a Roth IRA or a qualified rollover contribution from a designated Roth account which is a rollover contribution described in section 402A(c)(3)(A).

children.heading: Conversions
children.label: C Conversions
children.text: The conversion of an individual retirement plan (other than a Roth IRA) to a Roth IRA shall be treated for purposes of this paragraph as a distribution to which this paragraph applies.

children.heading: Additional reporting requirements
children.label: D Additional reporting requirements
children.text: Trustees of Roth IRAs, trustees of individual retirement plans, persons subject to section 6047(d)(1), or all of the foregoing persons, whichever is appropriate, shall include such additional information in reports required under section 408(i) or 6047 as the Secretary may require to ensure that amounts required to be included in gross income under subparagraph (A) are so included.

children.heading: Special rules for contributions to which 2-year averaging applies
children.label: E Special rules for contributions to which 2-year averaging applies

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The amount otherwise required to be included in gross income for any taxable year beginning in 2010 or the first taxable year in the 2-year period under subparagraph (A)(iii) shall be increased by the aggregate distributions from Roth IRAs for such taxable year which are allocable under paragraph (4) to the portion of such qualified rollover contribution required to be included in gross income under subparagraph (A)(i).

children.heading: Limitation on aggregate amount included
children.label: II Limitation on aggregate amount included
children.text: The amount required to be included in gross income for any taxable year under subparagraph (A)(iii) shall not exceed the aggregate amount required to be included in gross income under subparagraph (A)(iii) for all taxable years in the 2-year period (without regard to subclause (I)) reduced by amounts included for all preceding taxable years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: If the individual required to include amounts in gross income under such subparagraph dies before all of such amounts are included, all remaining amounts shall be included in gross income for the taxable year which includes the date of death.

children.heading: Special rule for surviving spouse
children.label: II Special rule for surviving spouse
children.text: If the spouse of the individual described in subclause (I) acquires the individual’s entire interest in any Roth IRA to which such qualified rollover contribution is properly allocable, the spouse may elect to treat the remaining amounts described in subclause (I) as includible in the spouse’s gross income in the taxable years of the spouse ending with or within the taxable years of such individual in which such amounts would otherwise have been includible. Any such election may not be made or changed after the due date for the spouse’s taxable year which includes the date of death.

children.heading: Special rule for applying section 72
children.label: F Special rule for applying section 72
I
children.text: any portion of a distribution from a Roth IRA is properly allocable to a qualified rollover contribution described in this paragraph; and

children.label: II
children.text: such distribution is made within the 5-taxable year period beginning with the taxable year in which such contribution was made,

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: Clause (i) shall apply only to the extent of the amount of the qualified rollover contribution includible in gross income under subparagraph (A)(i).

children.heading: Aggregation rules
children.label: A Aggregation rules
children.text: Section 408(d)(2) shall be applied separately with respect to Roth IRAs and other individual retirement plans.

children.label: i
children.text: from contributions to the extent that the amount of such distribution, when added to all previous distributions from the Roth IRA, does not exceed the aggregate contributions to the Roth IRA; and

children.label: I
children.text: Contributions other than qualified rollover contributions to which paragraph (3) applies.

children.label: II
children.text: Qualified rollover contributions to which paragraph (3) applies on a first-in, first-out basis.

children.heading: Qualified special purpose distribution
children.label: 5 Qualified special purpose distribution
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “qualified special purpose distribution” means any distribution to which subparagraph (F) of section 72(t)(2) applies.

children.heading: Taxpayer may make adjustments before due date
children.label: 6 Taxpayer may make adjustments before due date

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided by the Secretary, if, on or before the due date for any taxable year, a taxpayer transfers in a trustee-to-trustee transfer any contribution to an individual retirement plan made during such taxable year from such plan to any other individual retirement plan, then, for purposes of this chapter, such contribution shall be treated as having been made to the transferee plan (and not the transferor plan).

children.heading: Transfer of earnings
children.label: i Transfer of earnings
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to the transfer of any contribution unless such transfer is accompanied by any net income allocable to such contribution.

children.heading: No deduction
children.label: ii No deduction
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall apply to the transfer of any contribution only to the extent no deduction was allowed with respect to the contribution to the transferor plan.

children.heading: Conversions
children.label: iii Conversions
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply in the case of a qualified rollover contribution to which subsection (d)(3) applies (including by reason of subparagraph (C) thereof).

children.heading: Due date
children.label: 7 Due date
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the due date for any taxable year is the date prescribed by law (including extensions of time) for filing the taxpayer’s return for such taxable year.

children.label: A
children.text: to a Roth IRA from another such account,

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of an individual retirement plan, such rollover contribution meets the requirements of section 408(d)(3), and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of any eligible retirement plan (as defined in section 402(c)(8)(B) other than clauses (i) and (ii) thereof), such rollover contribution meets the requirements of section 402(c), 403(b)(8), or 457(e)(16), as applicable, and

children.label: C
children.text: from a qualified tuition program to the extent provided in section 529(c)(3)(E).For purposes of section 408(d)(3)(B), there shall be disregarded any qualified rollover contribution from an individual retirement plan (other than a Roth IRA) to a Roth IRA. The earnings and contributions of any qualified tuition program from which a qualified rollover contribution is made under subparagraph (C) shall be treated in the same manner as the earnings and contributions of a Roth IRA from which a qualified rollover contribution is made under subparagraph (A).

children.label: i
children.text: the sum of the amounts received during such period by such individual under such sections with respect to such person, reduced by

children.label: ii
children.text: the amounts so received which were contributed to a Coverdell education savings account under section 530(d)(9).

children.heading: Annual limit on number of rollovers not to apply
children.label: B Annual limit on number of rollovers not to apply
children.text: Section 408(d)(3)(B) shall not apply with respect to amounts treated as a rollover by subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Application of section 72
children.label: C Application of section 72
children.text: For purposes of applying section 72 in the case of a distribution which is not a qualified distribution, the amount treated as a rollover by reason of subparagraph (A) shall be treated as investment in the contract.

children.heading: Simple retirement accounts
children.label: 3 Simple retirement accounts
children.text: In the case of any payment or distribution out of a simple retirement account (as defined in section 408(p)) with respect to which an election has been made under section 408(p)(12) and to which 72(t)(6) applies, the term “qualified rollover contribution” shall not include any payment or distribution paid into an account other than another simple retirement account (as so defined).
